chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the artifact in the Acknowledgments section: https://zenodo.org/records/15283088, which contains the ReCraft code and Rocq proof sketches.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation was conducted on a public research cloud using up to 16 virtual machine (VM) instances. This constitutes a large-scale simulation/emulation environment rather than physical hardware testbeds or massive real-world deployments.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section III-A ('Failure Assumptions') contains a clear, explicit definition of the fault model, stating that ReCraft assumes an asynchronous network and non-Byzantine node failures, with specific tolerance formulas (f = n - q).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous data-driven analysis of trade-offs in Section VII. It quantifies performance overheads for split/merge operations (latency comparisons showing 21x improvement over baselines) and membership changes (consensus step counts), alongside throughput measurements.

major_weakness: The experimental evaluation relies on virtual machine simulations in a cloud environment rather than physical hardware testbeds or massive-scale real-world deployments.
